title: Onboarding wizard preset picker — solo-dev preset walks a new project to a working bridge

steps.title: Prerequisites
steps.body: Hub app running (npm run tauri dev in hub/). A Unity project with NO prior unity-open-mcp config (a fresh demo clone, or delete .unity-open-mcp/ + Packages/manifest.json unity-open-mcp entry before starting). The Unity Editor should NOT be open on the project yet — the wizard launches it.

steps.title: Open the onboarding wizard and pick the solo-dev preset
steps.body: In the Hub app, open the project. When the onboarding wizard appears, choose the 'Solo developer' preset. The preset selects: bridge auto-start, core + diagnostics + asset-intelligence groups on, no Hub CLI integration, auto-save dirty scenes enabled.

steps.title: Expected outcomes (preset applied)
items: The wizard writes .unity-open-mcp/settings.json with autoSaveDirtyScenes=true and the preset's default group set
The wizard offers to install the bridge package into the project (or detects it already installed)
The wizard offers to launch the Unity Editor on the project
No Hub CLI steps are shown (solo-dev preset skips Hub integration)

steps.title: Launch the Editor and confirm the bridge comes up
Expected outcomes (bridge up after wizard)
items: ping returns isError:false within ~30s of the Editor launching
capabilities kind:tools lists the preset's default groups (core, gate-and-verify, asset-intelligence, typed-editor, diagnostics)

steps.title: Reset the project config (so the scenario is re-runnable)
Paste the agent output (ping + editor_status + bridge_status envelopes)

reset.note: Optional scenario. The wizard preset picker is Hub UI; the post-wizard bridge-up confirmation is agent-driven (ping + editor_status + bridge_status). Re-runnable after deleting .unity-open-mcp/settings.json.
